description: Tracks sensory language usage across episodes to prevent 'sensory cloning' (repeating distinctive phrases)

his frame was wasted away to skin and bo.original: His frame was wasted away to skin and bone, his eyes sunken, his chest finally still.

vaporized into the dark machinery of gov.original: Vaporized into the dark machinery of government.

carved into the grain with a force that.original: carved into the grain with a force that almost tore the page

the birds have forgotten us. the sky is.original: The birds have forgotten us. The sky is empty. Even the flies do not land on the meat.

the grinding did not stop. it merely cha.original: The grinding did not stop. It merely changed frequency.

pupils did not contract. they remained f.original: pupils did not contract. They remained fully dilated. Wide open.

a sentence that stops the blood..original: a sentence that stops the blood.

and the current was beginning to flow..original: And the current was beginning to flow.

the universe has an immune system..original: The universe has an immune system.

the air had turned the color of a bruise.original: the air had turned the color of a bruise.

the audio track of the world had been mu.original: The audio track of the world had been muted.

a light so bright it cast shadows behind.original: a light so bright it cast shadows behind his eyes. He could see the veins in his own eyelids.

they were dithered together like a low-r.original: They were dithered together like a low-resolution image.

it is swallowing our memories. it is swa.original: It is swallowing our memories. It is swallowing our history.

heal the wound by burning out the infect.original: heal the wound by burning out the infection sites

phantom limb of a world that was amputat.original: phantom limb of a world that was amputated

we are the skin being stretched until it.original: We are the skin being stretched until it rips.

the incision has been examined. the reje.original: The incision has been examined. The rejection documented. The metastasis traced.

the immune system will eventually flush.original: The immune system will eventually flush the contaminant.

bliss watched the light paint the roofto.original: Bliss watched the light paint the rooftops of Boston in shades of wound and bruise.

the room pressed in from both sides???na.original: The room pressed in from both sides???narrow as a coffin stood on end.

this was bruised light. sick light. the.original: This was bruised light. Sick light. The color of a deep contusion spreading beneath the skin.

the surge came down the wire like a fist.original: The surge came down the wire like a fist.

the stylus was glowing cherry red. it wa.original: The stylus was glowing cherry red. It wasn't pressing. It was branding. The tip wasn't pressing anymore. It was eating.

this was arterial red. the red of someth.original: This was arterial red. The red of something opened.

a sky that looked like it had been flaye.original: a sky that looked like it had been flayed???the atmosphere peeled back to expose the red meat of something that should have stayed hidden.

the sea has lost its transparency. it lo.original: The sea has lost its transparency. It looks like milk.

the surface of the water looked like the.original: The surface of the water looked like the fluid in a blister.

the water behaved like gelatin that was.original: The water behaved like gelatin that was beginning to set.

and the physical rope was just the rotti.original: And the physical rope was just the rotting meat covering the bone. The light isn't illuminating the ship. It is examining it.

thousands of miles of copper wire strung.original: thousands of miles of copper wire strung across the continent like a nervous system laid bare.

the light pouring from that window was w.original: The light pouring from that window was white. Blinding, clinical white???the color of an operating theater, the color of a laboratory lamp focused on a specimen.

the ground brittle beneath him as if the.original: the ground brittle beneath him as if the earth itself had been dehydrated???every drop of moisture pulled upward, sacrificed to the column of white light drilling into his brother's nursery.

the grain buzzed against his fingertips.original: The grain buzzed against his fingertips like a hive.

a complex, many-sided form made of what.original: A complex, many-sided form made of what looked like frozen lightning???liquid and solid at once, its surfaces refracting the white glare into spectrums that had no names.

a seed pod bursting after planting..original: A seed pod bursting after planting.

then came the stitching. not a needle???.original: Then came the stitching. Not a needle???a sensation. The edges of his mind being pulled together. A seam closing behind his eyes.

the glow folded inward, drained back int.original: The glow folded inward, drained back into the crib like water down a pipe.

illuminated from outside like a stained.original: illuminated from outside like a stained glass window lit by a fire he couldn't escape.

a deep, earthy, wine-dark red. maroon. b.original: A deep, earthy, wine-dark red. Maroon. Burgundy. The color of velvet curtains in an old theater. The color of dried roses pressed between the pages of a book.

the reality you inhabit now is a clean r.original: The reality you inhabit now is a clean room. Sterile. Optimized for visibility. The examination lamp shows everything, hides nothing, leaves no corner unilluminated. But clean rooms are not built for living. They are built for surgery.

a residue the surgery tried to dissolve.original: A residue the surgery tried to dissolve but couldn't quite reach. A stain that set too deep. A thread of the old world woven so tightly into your neurons that cutting it out would mean cutting out you.

the red chartreuse you remember is a pha.original: The red Chartreuse you remember is a phantom limb.

it is the original code, still running b.original: It is the original code, still running beneath the scar tissue, still trying to render a world that has been overwritten.

the pages were feathered at the edges fr.original: The pages were feathered at the edges from a thousand turnings.

the gothic script was dense and black, e.original: The Gothic script was dense and black, each letter pressed into the page by machinery that had been dead for decades.

the wound had closed, but the scar was v.original: The wound had closed, but the scar was visible.

the grid???the faint grey lattice that h.original: The grid???the faint grey lattice that had aggressively etched itself into his vision in the cellar???flickered at the edges of his sight.

the sun hammered down on him. the heat p.original: The sun hammered down on him. The heat pressed against his skull.

the red chartreuse you remember is a pha.original: The red Chartreuse you remember is a phantom limb. It is your optic nerve trying to process a color that no longer exists in this lighting environment.

the art was residue. it was the fossil r.original: The art was residue. It was the fossil record of the old world.

the scar tissue had already grown over t.original: The scar tissue had already grown over the wound in his memory.

you are the residue. you are the fossil.original: You are the residue. You are the fossil record. You are the lion in a world that insists there have only ever been wolves. Watch the colors bleeding out of the sky???the oranges, the purples, the deep reds that the old light used to cast. A residue the surgery tried to dissolve but couldn't quite reach. A stain that set too deep. A thread of the old world woven so tightly into your neurons that cutting it out would mean cutting out you. Something that marks you as incompatible.

watch the colors bleeding out of the sky.original: Watch the colors bleeding out of the sky???the oranges, the purples, the deep reds that the old light used to cast.

the light on the floorboards had turned.original: The light on the floorboards had turned violet.

'a woman in a grey dress was weeping, he.original: 'A woman in a grey dress was weeping, her hands raised toward a sky'

'the machines in the office continued th.original: 'The machines in the office continued their percussion. Three strikes. Pause. Six strikes. Pause. Nine strikes.'

baking under an invisible noon sun, thou.original: baking under an invisible noon sun, though the hour was closer to midnight.

the nursery was glowing. but not red. th.original: The nursery was glowing. But not red. The light pouring from that window was white. Blinding, clinical white???the color of an operating theater, the color of a laboratory lamp focused on a specimen.

the stone was cool against his back..original: The stone was cool against his back.

the air turned thick and bright..original: the air turned thick and bright.

the letters are legible. the dots and da.original: The letters are legible. The dots and dashes have been seared into the fiber, their edges feathered where the heat fluctuated.

the stone steps were cool beneath his ba.original: The stone steps were cool beneath his bare feet.

amber body. brown mane. head resting on.original: Amber body. Brown mane. Head resting on the white wool of the lamb beside it.

the radiation was beating down on his sc.original: The radiation was beating down on his scalp, crisping the fine dark hair.

bottomless in a way that had nothing to.original: Bottomless in a way that had nothing to do with the color of the iris.

his small finger aimed directly at the w.original: His small finger aimed directly at the white hole in the sky, unflinching.

the examination lamp shows everything, h.original: The examination lamp shows everything, hides nothing, leaves no corner unilluminated.

no bird song. no insect hum. just the so.original: No bird song. No insect hum. Just the sound of the wind moving through the dry grass, and the sound of the Tesla house breathing.

it is the sound of a machine trying to f.original: It is the sound of a machine trying to force two gears to mesh when the teeth don't align.

that is the sound of the fabric tearing.original: That is the sound of the fabric tearing between the staples.

cracked the brass arm against the anvil.original: cracked the brass arm against the anvil with the sound of a pistol shot.

they moved like something alive???like t.original: They moved like something alive???like the slow contraction of muscle, like the rhythm of a massive heart pushing blood through vessels too large to comprehend.

begging the sky for mercy in a voice tha.original: begging the sky for mercy in a voice that cracked and broke like kindling.

the air inside the house tasted of light.original: The air inside the house tasted of lightning. Static charge crackled through every surface.

he sighed???a small, satisfied sound, li.original: He sighed???a small, satisfied sound, like a child who had completed a recitation???satisfied not with play, but with performance. With duty discharged.

the sound echoed through the empty churc.original: The sound echoed through the empty church like a gunshot.

the sounder began to hammer. not morse c.original: The sounder began to hammer. Not Morse code. Not letters or numbers. A pulse. A rhythm. Three strikes, pause. Three strikes, pause.

'his heart stuttered. skipped. then lock.original: 'His heart stuttered. Skipped. Then locked into a rhythm that wasn't his'

colors looked the same but landed differ.original: Colors looked the same but landed differently. Sounds arrived without the cushion of distance.

voice that cracked and broke like kindli.original: voice that cracked and broke like kindling.

a low hum filled the hallway, too deep t.original: A low hum filled the hallway, too deep to hear properly???more pressure than sound, pressing against his eardrums from the inside.

the beam of light hummed???a sound so lo.original: The beam of light hummed???a sound so low it was almost below hearing, more vibration than tone.

charred, then soaked in the humidity of.original: Charred, then soaked in the humidity of a century, then dried again into a brittleness that feels like a warning.

the spark jumped to his thumb.original: the spark jumped to his thumb

his footsteps echoed against the stone..original: His footsteps echoed against the stone.

a heavy volume, bound in leather that ha.original: A heavy volume, bound in leather that had cracked with age.

his voice was clear. no toddler's lisp..original: His voice was clear. No toddler's lisp. No fumbling for words. The sentence came out whole, precise, as if it had been rehearsed.

the body wasn't even cold yet..original: The body wasn't even cold yet.

they swept through the two small rooms l.original: They swept through the two small rooms like locusts.

it wasn't radiant heat. it didn't warm h.original: It wasn't radiant heat. It didn't warm his skin. It burned his shirt off his back.

another smell threaded through???sharp,.original: another smell threaded through???sharp, metallic, the taste of a split battery leaking acid onto the tongue.

the atmosphere had thickened into someth.original: The atmosphere had thickened into something that didn't want to be walked through.

the shadow rope was crisp. defined. perf.original: The shadow rope was crisp. Defined. Perfect. It looked like an X-ray. It looked like the light was shining through the rope and revealing the structure underneath.

dragging through his veins like syrup th.original: dragging through his veins like syrup through a straw.

the beam cut through the red fog of the.original: The beam cut through the red fog of the yard like a scalpel through the swollen meat of a wound.

the air carried the chill of void???the.original: The air carried the chill of void???the absolute cold of a place where no star had ever burned.

the light screaming over the mountains w.original: The light screaming over the mountains was the white of a welding arc???furious, chemical, edgeless. A hole in the sky where color went to die. The rays didn't warm the valley. They bleached it. The grass lost its green. The red tiles of the church roof turned the brown of dried blood. The world looked like a photograph left too long in the sun???all the pigment bleached out, leaving only the skeleton of what had been.

the skin had closed over the entry point.original: The skin had closed over the entry points like water healing over a stone's splashâ€”smooth, pink, and utterly foreign.

his shadow stretched before him\u00e2\u2.original: His shadow stretched before himâ€”thin, grey, the edges blurred. He looked down at it. The shadow looked like it was fading. Like the light was passing through him instead of being blocked by him.

he swallowed the tears the way he swallo.original: he swallowed the tears the way he swallowed everything now???down into the place where the cold wave had passed through, where the stitching had closed, where the things he couldn't say were beginning to pile up like stones.

chartreuse is a screaming, radioactive y.original: Chartreuse is a screaming, radioactive yellow-green. The color of a highlighter. The color of antifreeze. The color of something that wants to be seen from a great distance, that has no interest in subtlety or warmth. It is named after a French liqueur that has been this shade of acid-green since seventeen thirty-seven.

'the shadow of the rigging was cast on t.original: 'The shadow of the rigging was cast on the white planks of the deck. Every rope. Every knot. Every frayed fiber was visible. The shadow was sharper than the object casting it.'

the iron latch on the front door seared.original: The iron latch on the front door seared his palm even through the hem of his nightshirt. The skin blistered, but he didn't cry out. He twisted the handle and kicked the door open.

the red light passed through his skin. h.original: The red light passed through his skin. His fingers had gone translucent. He could see the dark architecture of his bones, the black web of veins branching beneath the surface. The blood moving through those vessels had slowed. He could feel it???thick, reluctant, dragging through his veins like syrup through a straw.

heat pressed down on him. not the warmth.original: Heat pressed down on him. Not the warmth of fire???this was dry, penetrating, a heat that started in his marrow and radiated outward through the muscle.

the iron latch on the front door seared.original: The iron latch on the front door seared his palm even through the hem of his nightshirt. The skin blistered, but he didn't cry out.

the wood was warm. the grain buzzed agai.original: The wood was warm. The grain buzzed against his fingertips like a hive.

the beam hit him like cold water..original: The beam hit him like cold water.

the wax was melting. it dripped over his.original: The wax was melting. It dripped over his fingers in slow rivulets. Soft. Warm. Pooling in the creases of his palm. The cellar temperature was fifty degrees. Cold enough to see his breath if he exhaled sharply. Wax melts at one hundred and forty. There was no flame. No heat source.

the candle wax had cooled. it pooled aro.original: The candle wax had cooled. It pooled around his boots in a white ring, hard and smooth, the texture of old bone.

the paper is brown. not the warm sepia o.original: The paper is brown. Not the warm sepia of aged documents???the brown of old blood, of coffee grounds left too long in the filter.

the edges crumble when touched. flakes o.original: The edges crumble when touched. Flakes of carbon drift onto the metal table.

the stylus pressed clean indentations in.original: The stylus pressed clean indentations into the fiber. Dots and dashes, compressed but intact. The spacing is regular. The pressure is uniform.

the mark isn't on the paper. it's throug.original: The mark isn't on the paper. It's through the paper. A canyon cut by a tool that had become a weapon.

you feel the difference. you feel the nu.original: You feel the difference. You feel the numbness. You feel the absence of something that used to be there.

the binding was soft from use. the pages.original: The binding was soft from use. The pages were feathered at the edges from a thousand turnings.

the paper felt slick. smooth. the textur.original: The paper felt slick. Smooth. The texture of old vellum should have caught on his fingertip, the tooth of the fiber resisting his touch. This page offered no resistance. His finger slid across it as if the surface had been sealed???laminated by something that had come after the printing.

the paper was cold. not cool, the way st.original: The paper was cold. Not cool, the way stone is cool in a cellar. Cold like dead skin. Cold like something that had stopped circulating.

the white sun was drilling through it no.original: The white sun was drilling through it now, washing out the colors, turning the amber lion into a pale orange blur.

lighter, sharper, more efficient, more n.original: lighter, sharper, more efficient, more numb

screaming, radioactive yellow-green. the.original: screaming, radioactive yellow-green. The color of a highlighter. The color of antifreeze. The color of something that wants to be seen from a great distance, that has no interest in subtlety or warmth.

grey, watery separation that smelled of.original: grey, watery separation that smelled of ozone

the air smells of salt and diesel and we.original: The air smells of salt and diesel and welding smoke.

the air smelled of stale incense and som.original: The air smelled of stale incense and something elseâ€”the metallic residue of the storm, the lingering ozone of a lightning strike that had hit somewhere far away but left its signature in every enclosed space. It smelled like a welding shop inside a sanctuary.

a spark leapt from his ear to his should.original: a spark leapt from his ear to his shoulder???a bright snap that left the smell of singed hair.

the room smells of vinegar and old dust..original: The room smells of vinegar and old dust.

the air smelled of stale incense and som.original: The air smelled of stale incense and something else???the metallic residue of the storm, the lingering ozone of a lightning strike that had hit somewhere far away but left its signature in every enclosed space.

the valley had drowned in red light, and.original: The valley had drowned in red light, and the air hung heavy with the taste of copper and wet ash.

the air tasted of dust and ??? stone..original: The air tasted of dust and ??? stone.

the words tasted strange in his mouth..original: The words tasted strange in his mouth.
